Petition For Instructions And Order For: 1) Settlement Of First Account And Report (1-3-13--12-31-15) Of Successor Trustee; 2) Confirmation Of Trust Debt To Dara Wong, Individually, For Loans To The Trust During The Accounting Period; 3) Declaratory Relief Regarding Trust Debts To Petitioner Preceding The Accounting Period; And 4) Approval Of Trustee Fees For The Accounting Period [Prob. 17200]
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Continued To Nov-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ross Madden for and with Dara Wong.
Margaret O'Dowd for and with Jolene Huey (Beneficiary).
Lily Moallem for Kathleen Wong (Beneficiary).

Mr. Madden stated that they have a mediated settlement agreement, and that the opposing side believes his Client misrepresented whether she was living at the premises so the settlement agreement should be invalidated. Ms. Moallem stated that the grounds for set aside was that the Trustee failed to meet any of the deadlines, and that if the Trustee lived in the house for four years and or stored her belongings there, she owes back rent, or a surcharge for leaving the units vacant. Mr. Madden stated that there are two checks in opposing Counsel's hands which were delivered one week late. The Petition for Instructions and Order for Settlement of First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is continued to November 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an evidentiary hearing. Trial briefs, exhibit lists and witness lists are due by November 9, 2017.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions And Order For: 1) Settlement Of First Account And Report (1-3-13--12-31-15) Of Successor Trustee; 2) Confirmation Of Trust Debt To Dara Wong, Individually, For Loans To The Trust During The Accounting Period; 3) Declaratory Relief Regarding Trust Debts To Petitioner Preceding The Accounting Period; And 4) Approval Of Trustee Fees For The Accounting Period [Prob. 17200]
Off Calendar Sep-07-2016 Continued To Nov-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dave Parnall for and with (petitioner) Dara Wong. Lily Moallem for and with (beneficiary) Kathleen Wong. Margaret O'Dowd for Jolene Huey.

The Court addressed Ms. O'Dowd asking if they were ready for mediation and trial dates. Ms. O'Dowd stated that mediation was fine. Mr. Parnall stated that it is premature for a trial or mediation date, that they are here on an accounting which is not objected to, that the loans to Dara Wong have not been objected to, that they just received the response, that there has been no telephone call to raise the issues, that the issues are not properly before the Court and that the attorneys should talk first to figure out the issues. The Court asked if they want additional time to work things out. Mr. Parnall stated that this is ready to be submitted on the documents and that there is no dispute on the post death loans. Ms. Moallem stated that they are amenable to mediation, that they responded to the proprietary reimbursements, that they are subject to surcharge, that the current accounting needs to be continued or they need to set mediation/trial and that they will need up to 90 days for discussions. Ms. O'Dowd stated that 60 days should be sufficient and that the house should be sold immediately. Ms. Moallem stated that they want the house sold and that confirmation of sale should not be conducted by anyone associated with the trust. Mr. Parnall stated that there are conflicts regarding the reimbursements, that he is not clear of their positions, that there is no petition regarding a surcharge, that there have been no attempts to talk, that regarding the sale of the property it is now known that they want it sold and asked if the Court wants further briefing. Ms. O'Dowd stated that in regards to the accounting there are no utilities listed and that they want to see the invoices. Mr. Parnall stated that this is the first request for such. The Court stated that it will continue the matter to allow time for informal discussions and asked if anything can be resolved today. Ms. Moallem stated that she hesitates to say that anything could be resolved today and Ms. O'Dowd agreed. Mr. Parnall stated that this has been pending for 5 months and they are now coming in with objections. The Court continued the Petition for Instructions, etc., to November 7, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and set a deadline of October 24, 2016 for filing anything additional.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Accounting Petition
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Off Calendar

Petition To Compel Dara Wong To Provide Trust Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Margaret O'Dowd for and with Jolene Huey. Dara Wong (beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this is a petition to compel Dara to file an accounting, that it was to be pre-granted and asked if Ms. Wong is here to object. Ms. Wong stated that she objects to the tentative ruling and that she wants a 2-3 month continuance to obtain counsel and that she has started on the accounting. The Court asked if she was objecting to the accounting or just to the time frame. Ms. Wong responded that it is the timing. The Court stated that it would order the accounting but set a reasonable time frame. Ms. Wong stated that she wants her attorney to respond, that she has not signed the fee agreement yet, that it will take 2 months to provide the accounting and that she would like the attorney to file a response. The Court told Ms. Wong that she would be spending money to file a response when she already said she would file the accounting. Ms. Wong stated that she lodged a will this morning. The Court stated that that is not relevant, that it does not change the need for an accounting, that it will go ahead and sign the order, that all it does is require an accounting in two months and that it does not impose anything other than what needs to happen. The Court granted the petition to compel Dara Wong to provide a trust accounting and signed the order in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
